steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The left side is an independent clause ending with a blank that needs a noun object (to complete “craft a 10-by-13-inch ___”). The right side is an independent clause beginning with “in 2014, she arranged.”

steps.title: Apply the joining rule
steps.content:
Choice B—”sampler;”—is correct because a semicolon joins two independent clauses when there is no coordinating conjunction.

The other options fail by using only a comma (creating a comma splice) or by adding “later,” which contradicts the specific date “in 2014.” This matches choice B — B is correct.
